title: Schemas and Validation Rules
purpose: Define the authoritative JSON Schema contracts and repository validation rules that support the standard without prescribing one service or database.
scope: This specification covers the authoritative JSON Schema package, repository-wide catalog validation, generated evidence snapshot contracts, inline identifier reference handling inside canonical JSON string fields, and the validation capabilities expected from tooling.
context: The standard depends on compact requirement records, explicit trace links, lightweight upstream lineage, and repository-level graph checks. Tooling needs an authoritative schema and a repository-wide catalog so references behave like foreign keys instead of loose prose.

requirements.title: Provide schemas for the core extracted shapes
requirements.statement: The reference package MUST provide authoritative JSON Schema definitions for canonical artifact metadata, identifier policy metadata, compact requirement records, requirement trace fields, generated evidence snapshots, and work-item trace fields.

requirements.title: Keep front matter validation strict by document family
requirements.statement: The authoritative JSON Schema package MUST validate `artifact_type`, status vocabularies, and artifact identifier patterns for each document family.
notes: Optional local extension fields may appear in canonical JSON artifacts, but the core family-specific keys stay fixed.

requirements.title: Validate requirement identifiers as part of the identifier catalog
requirements.statement: The authoritative identifier definitions MUST validate both artifact identifier rules and `REQ-...` identifier rules.

requirements.title: Validate the compact requirement clause shape
requirements.statement: The canonical requirement definition MUST validate a `REQ-...` identifier, short title, a normative clause that contains exactly one approved uppercase keyword, and optional trace or notes data.
notes: The clause schema enforces the narrowed BCP 14-style uppercase keyword set used by the standard.

requirements.title: Keep requirement trace labels canonical
requirements.statement: The canonical requirement trace definition MUST allow only `satisfied_by`, `implemented_by`, `verified_by`, `derived_from`, `supersedes`, `upstream_refs`, and `related`.
notes: `satisfied_by` accepts ARC IDs, `implemented_by` accepts WI IDs, `verified_by` accepts VER IDs, `derived_from` and `supersedes` accept REQ IDs, `upstream_refs` remains a free-form string array, and `related` may mix requirement IDs and other core artifact IDs.

requirements.title: Treat schemas as extracted-shape contracts
requirements.statement: The authoritative schema layer MUST describe canonical JSON artifact shapes rather than require one specific projection or parser implementation.

requirements.title: Resolve explicit traceability links
requirements.statement: Validation tooling MUST be able to resolve requirement links to design, work-item, verification, and lineage identifiers when those references are present in canonical JSON metadata.

requirements.title: Report traceability gaps
requirements.statement:
Validation tooling SHOULD be able to report requirements missing design,
implementation, verification, or requested reporting coverage dimensions.

requirements.title: Keep evidence kinds extensible and schema-agnostic
requirements.statement:
The evidence snapshot schema MUST model evidence kinds as lowercase extensible
tokens rather than as a language-specific or framework-specific grammar.

requirements.title: Surface evidence coverage views
requirements.statement:
Validation tooling SHOULD be able to report which requirements have observed
evidence for the well-known and custom evidence kinds present in evaluated
evidence snapshots.

requirements.title: Constrain trace-bearing identifier families
requirements.statement: The authoritative JSON Schema definitions MUST constrain the direct trace-bearing lists in top-level artifact fields and requirement or work-item trace blocks to the identifier family each field is meant to carry rather than accept generic non-empty strings.

requirements.title: Detect duplicate identifiers
requirements.statement: Validation tooling MUST detect duplicate artifact IDs and duplicate requirement IDs in the repository.

requirements.title: Resolve explicit trace references
requirements.statement: Validation tooling MUST resolve explicit trace references and report unresolved requirement or artifact references in direct trace-bearing fields.

requirements.title: Enforce reciprocal trace consistency
requirements.statement: Repository tooling MUST report when a requirement link is not reciprocated by the linked architecture, work-item, or verification artifact.

requirements.title: Keep namespaces aligned
requirements.statement: Validation tooling MUST report when an artifact's canonical domain value or identifier namespace does not align with the directory namespace that owns the file.

requirements.title: Model lineage and upstream refs explicitly
requirements.statement: The authoritative JSON Schema trace definition MUST model `derived_from` and `supersedes` as requirement-reference arrays and `upstream_refs` as a non-empty string array.

requirements.title: Allow lineage references without tombstones
requirements.statement: Validation tooling MUST accept `Derived From` and `Supersedes` references even when the referenced requirement IDs are not present as active documents in the repository.

requirements.title: Document inline identifier references in schema contracts
requirements.statement: The authoritative schema package and validation guidance MUST document inline identifier references as backtick-delimited canonical artifact identifiers whose full resolution may require repository-level validation beyond JSON shape checking alone.
notes: Inline identifier references are prose links, not `trace`-block fields.
JSON Schema can describe the record shape, but it cannot fully resolve cross-file links by itself.
Repository-level tooling can extract and validate inline references against the repository's known artifact and retired-ID catalogs.

requirements.title: Prevent trace inference from inline identifier references
requirements.statement: Validation tooling MUST NOT infer typed trace edges from inline identifier references alone.
notes: Inline references can be reported as mentions, but they are not a substitute for structured trace data.

requirements.title: Extract inline identifier references when practical
requirements.statement: Validation tooling SHOULD extract inline identifier references separately from structured trace fields when it can do so.
notes: Separate extraction supports lightweight mention reporting without promoting prose references into graph edges.

requirements.title: Extract inline identifiers from canonical JSON string fields
requirements.statement: Validation tooling MUST extract canonical identifiers from inline prose references in canonical JSON string fields when the visible identifier text is the canonical token.
notes: This keeps inline identifier references compatible with canonical JSON authoring.
Derived browsing views may add anchors or links for navigation without changing the extracted identifier.
Extractors should normalize only the surrounding projection syntax, not the identifier text itself.

requirements.title: Surface reporting dimensions for downstream tooling
requirements.statement:
Validation tooling SHOULD report per-repository and per-requirement coverage
for `Upstream Refs`, `Satisfied By`, `Implemented By`, `Verified By`, and the
evidence kinds present in evaluated evidence snapshots.
notes: These dimension reports complement the profile result instead of replacing it.
Coverage views help teams distinguish greenfield gaps from brownfield trace debt.

requirements.title: Emit machine-readable coverage summaries when requested
requirements.statement: Validation tooling SHOULD be able to emit machine-readable summaries of coverage dimensions for dashboards, attestation tooling, and repository reporting.

requirements.title: Provide a schema for generated evidence snapshots
requirements.statement: The evidence snapshot contract MUST validate generated evidence snapshots including producer metadata, requirement IDs, evidence kinds, statuses, and optional refs.

requirements.title: Allow partial evidence snapshots
requirements.statement:
Validation tooling MUST accept evidence snapshots that cover only a subset of
requirements or only a subset of evidence kinds.

requirements.title: Merge overlapping evidence snapshots additively when reporting
requirements.statement:
Validation tooling SHOULD merge overlapping evidence snapshots additively when
it produces repository-level or requirement-level evidence reports.

requirements.title: Avoid negative inference from single-snapshot omission
requirements.statement:
Validation tooling MUST NOT infer that evidence is absent from the repository
solely because one evaluated evidence snapshot omits a requirement or evidence
kind.
